inlineContent.text: The keyboard layout of a software keyboard can vary between locales and devices, which means that a layout engine must be flexible.
In KeyboardKit, a
is used to define the full set of keys of a keyboard. A layout can be created from an
, which defines only the input keys. This allows us to reuse identical layouts for many compatible input sets.
KeyboardKit defines some input sets like
, and
, as well as a standard keyboard layout that is compatible with
and similar input sets.
👑 KeyboardKit Pro unlocks more input sets, like
and
, and localized multi-device layouts for all supported locales. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the
KeyboardKit has a
type that defines a full keyboard layout. It’s also a namespace for other layout-related types, like
, which has a
function that returns a standard configuration for a certain context.
In KeyboardKit an
specifies the input keys of a keyboard. KeyboardKit comes with a few input sets, like
, and
, while KeyboardKit Pro unlocks many more, like
, etc.
A
can be generated from an alphabetic, numeric, and symbolic input set. Certain locales support multiple
s and can be added multiple times with different layouts, like
, etc.
The
will automatically apply the default layout for the current
, and many other parameters. You can inject a custom
if you for instance want to adjust certain keys.
KeyboardKit can generate a
layout for the current
. This makes it easy to adjust the standard layout, using layout extensions like
, etc.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks more input sets, like
and
, as well as localized input sets and layouts for all
locales that are included in your license.
You can access localized layouts with the throwing builders. like
. These builders will throw a license error if you try to access them without a valid license. If you are on the Basic or Silver plan, you must specify which locales to use in your
KeyboardKit Pro extends
with even more functionality, to make it easier to add, remove, and replace items. See the
documentation and its nested
and
types for a full list of additional capabilities.
KeyboardKit Pro extends
with a list of
and makes the
add a layout picker to all locales that support multiple layouts.
This means languages like
can be added many times, with different layouts. The following locales support multiple layouts:
All localized layout implementations use the
to adjust their alphabetic input set for the current layout type.

abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit layout engine.
A list of layout types that the locale supports.
A list of KeyboardKit supported locales.
A standard QWERTY input set.
This is a QWERTZ alphabetic input set.
A keyboard layout items defines an action, size, and
optional insets for a key on a layout-based keyboard.
This type defines layout configs for various devices.
This article describes the KeyboardKit external keyboard engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit feedback engine.
The standard config for the provided
The current locale, by default
This article describes the KeyboardKit callout engine.
This enum defines supported keyboard layout types.
A standard numeric input set.
This article describes the KeyboardKit navigation engine.
The standard keyboard layout to use for the provided
keyboard context and input set.
This article describes the KeyboardKit dictation engine.
A standard symbolic input set.
This article describes the KeyboardKit autocomplete engine.
This view renders a view that mimics the native keyboard.
This is a Colemak alphabetic input set.
This is an Dvorak alphabetic input set.
A QWERTY keyboard layout type.
This article describes the KeyboardKit localization engine.
This type can be used to define important properties for
your app, and is also a namespace for app-based features.
Insert an item before an action in all rows.
The device type that is currently being used.
Remove a certain action from all rows.
This article describes the KeyboardKit AI support capabilities.
This article describes the KeyboardKit input engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit emoji engine.
This article describes how KeyboardKit Pro lets you define and use a host application.
This article describes KeyboardKit’s clipboard support.
This article describes KeyboardKit’s custom font support.
This type defines the full set of keys on a keyboard and
is also a namespace for layout-related types.
This article describes the KeyboardKit app-specific utilities.
This article links to the various KeyboardKit features.
This article describes the KeyboardKit action engine.
This screen can be used as the main keyboard locales
screen in your app or keyboard extension.
A keyboard layout input set defines which input keys
to add to a keyboard layout.
A Dvorak keyboard layout type.
This is an AZERTY alphabetic input set.
The keyboard input type that is currently used.
The current keyboard layout type, if any.
A Colemak keyboard layout type.
KeyboardKit helps you create fully customizable keyboard extensions, using Swift and SwiftUI.
This class has observable states and persistent settings
for the core keyboard functionality.
This typealias represents a list of layout items.
